articles.title: Cable news network MS NOW shuffles schedule, shortens ‘Morning Joe’
articles.description:
The “Morning Joe” crew will be on for three hours starting at 6 a.m., instead of four, with MS NOW saying it was the show's choice to cut back.

The post Cable news network MS NOW shuffles schedule, shortens ‘Morning Joe’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MS NOW shortens 'Morning Joe' to three hours, starting at 6 a.m.
The decision to cut back was made by the show's crew.
Boston residents will see less morning news coverage starting March 2026.

articles.title: Newly discovered ‘Whitey’ Bulger writings show former FBI agent was framed, lawyers allege
articles.description:
“I never thought the day would come that I’d be writing a story about my criminal activity,” Bulger wrote.

The post Newly discovered ‘Whitey’ Bulger writings show former FBI agent was framed, lawyers allege appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lawyers claim newly found writings suggest Whitey Bulger was framed by former FBI agents.
Bulger's writings detail his criminal activities, revealing insights into Boston's crime history.
This revelation may impact ongoing discussions about law enforcement accountability in Boston.

articles.title: Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son
articles.description:
Babcock is accused of squeezing the child, laying his chest on him, and punching him in the back, fracturing his ribs, femurs, and clavicle.

The post Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nashua man, Babcock, arrested for allegedly assaulting his 3-month-old son.
Child suffered fractured ribs, femurs, and clavicle due to the assault.
Local authorities emphasize the need for child protection in the community.

articles.title: More than 150,000 uncounted COVID-19 deaths occurred early in the pandemic, a study finds
articles.description:
“Our antiquated death investigation system is one key reason why we fell short of accurate counts, particularly outside of big metropolitan areas,” said Andrew Stokes of Boston University.

The post More than 150,000 uncounted COVID-19 deaths occurred early in the pandemic, a study finds appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Study reveals over 150,000 uncounted COVID-19 deaths occurred early in the pandemic.
Andrew Stokes from Boston University highlights flaws in death investigation systems.
Inaccurate death counts may affect public health policies and resource allocation in Boston.
